props.ChoiceText: Months of repetitive labor taught you how much of mastery lives in strain, rhythm, and doing the same hard thing without complaint.
props.ChoiceBio: Workshop drudgery hardened your habits, your hands, and your patience long before it improved your reputation.

0.TransitionText: Because workshop drudgery hardened your habits and made patience part of your craft, your life stayed with tools, labor, and the discipline of making things well.
0.TransitionPrompt: This could carry you into Craftsman, but you may remain on your current path instead.

2.TargetText: Repetition left your hands thickened, your back familiar with strain, and your body marked by the labor behind good work.
